etymology.description: ใกญ(ใกญ) (jรฌ, ancient form of ็นผ) originally depicted four ๅนบ(ๅนบ) (yฤo, "tiny") representing silk or threads
etymology.interpretation: The character ใกญ originally was an ancient form that visually represented the idea of silk threads, which aligns with its later semantic role in ็นผ(็นผ) (jรฌ, "continue") as part of its phono-semantic composition with ็ณธ(็ณธ) ("silk; thread") indicating things about continuity.
